hypothesis: Anchoring the CTA stage to a specific Northern Ontario seasonal date will push draft quality from 9 toward 10/10. Evidence: all 3 kept experiments gained score by adding NO-specific context at each stage (lede, then hook, then lede fact). The CMI body structure CTA step (exp_5 addition) prescribes a real timeline but leaves urgency vague. Hypothesis: naming a specific Northern Ontario seasonal date in or near the CTA (e.g. walleye opener May 17, ice-out late April, fall freeze-up November, snowblower season October) creates a time-bounded reason to act that is both locally credible and conversion-relevant. This is the same NO-specificity principle applied to the CTA layer. Note: this is null #3 candidate — if gate-blocked again, escalate to analyst per feedback_autoresearch_gate_dependent_metric.md.
learning: NULL/gate-blocked outcome — null #3. Service-page standby still in effect; no content produced during the 48h window. Seasonal urgency CTA hypothesis (NO-specific date anchor in CTA) is sound in theory but unmeasurable while the gate holds. Surface was trimmed to CMI-only by user after creation — surface additions not persisting. Per feedback_autoresearch_gate_dependent_metric.md: 3 consecutive nulls triggers analyst escalation. Escalating now.
changes_description: Added seasonal CTA rule to surface: CTA must name a specific Northern Ontario seasonal date/deadline (walleye opener, ice-out, snowblower season, etc.) tied to the product topic. Replaces generic CTAs with time-bounded urgency. Builds on exp_3 lede (NO-specific fact) + exp_5 CMI body structure. Null #3 candidate — will escalate to analyst if gate-blocked again.
